items.verse: دَرَجَـٰتٍۢ مِّنْهُ وَمَغْفِرَةًۭ وَرَحْمَةًۭ ۚ وَكَانَ ٱللَّهُ غَفُورًۭا رَّحِيمًا
items.verseEn: Degrees [of high position] from Him and forgiveness and mercy. And Allāh is ever Forgiving and Merciful.

items.verse: وَجَزَٰٓؤُا۟ سَيِّئَةٍۢ سَيِّئَةٌۭ مِّثْلُهَا ۖ فَمَنْ عَفَا وَأَصْلَحَ فَأَجْرُهُۥ عَلَى ٱللَّهِ ۚ إِنَّهُۥ لَا يُحِبُّ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And the retribution for an evil act is an evil one like it, but whoever pardons and makes reconciliation - his reward is [due] from Allāh. Indeed, He does not like wrongdoers.

items.verse: وَدَّ كَثِيرٌۭ مِّنْ أَهْلِ ٱلْكِتَـٰبِ لَوْ يَرُدُّونَكُم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ إِيمَـٰنِكُمْ كُفَّارًا حَسَدًۭا مِّنْ عِندِ أَنفُسِهِم مِّنۢ بَعْدِ مَا تَبَيَّنَ لَهُمُ ٱلْحَقُّ ۖ فَٱعْفُوا۟ وَٱصْفَحُوا۟ حَتَّىٰ يَأْتِىَ ٱللَّهُ بِأَمْرِهِۦٓ ۗ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ عَلَىٰ كُلِّ شَىْءٍۢ قَدِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: Many of the People of the Scripture wish they could turn you back to disbelief after you have believed, out of envy from themselves [even] after the truth has become clear to them. So pardon and overlook until Allāh delivers His command. Indeed, Allāh is over all things competent.

items.verse: وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ وَعَمِلُوا۟ ٱلصَّـٰلِحَـٰتِ وَءَامَنُوا۟ بِمَا نُزِّلَ عَلَىٰ مُحَمَّدٍۢ وَهُوَ ٱلْحَقُّ مِن رَّبِّهِمْ ۙ كَفَّرَ عَنْهُمْ سَيِّـَٔاتِهِمْ وَأَصْلَحَ بَالَهُمْ
items.verseEn: And those who believe and do righteous deeds and believe in what has been sent down upon Muḥammad - and it is the truth from their Lord - He will remove from them their misdeeds and amend their condition.

items.verse: فَـَٔامِنُوا۟ بِٱللَّهِ وَرَسُولِهِۦ وَٱلنُّورِ ٱلَّذِىٓ أَنزَلْنَا ۚ وَٱللَّهُ بِمَا تَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌۭ
items.verseEn: So believe in Allāh and His Messenger and the light [i.e., the Qur’ān] which We have sent down. And Allāh is Aware of what you do.

items.verse: قُلْ أَرَءَيْتُمْ إِن جَعَلَ ٱللَّهُ عَلَيْكُمُ ٱلَّيْلَ سَرْمَدًا إِلَىٰ يَوْمِ ٱلْقِيَـٰمَةِ مَنْ إِلَـٰهٌ غَيْرُ ٱللَّهِ يَأْتِيكُم بِضِيَآءٍ ۖ أَفَلَا تَسْمَعُونَ
items.verseEn: Say, "Have you considered: if Allāh should make for you the night continuous until the Day of Resurrection, what deity other than Allāh could bring you light? Then will you not hear?"

items.verse: فَقَضَىٰهُنَّ سَبْعَ سَمَـٰوَاتٍۢ فِى يَوْمَيْنِ وَأَوْحَىٰ فِى كُلِّ سَمَآءٍ أَمْرَهَا ۚ وَزَيَّنَّا ٱلسَّمَآءَ ٱلدُّنْيَا بِمَصَـٰبِيحَ وَحِفْظًۭا ۚ ذَٰلِكَ تَقْدِيرُ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْعَلِيمِ
items.verseEn: And He completed them as seven heavens within two days and inspired [i.e., made known] in each heaven its command. And We adorned the nearest heaven with lamps [i.e., stars, for beauty] and as protection. That is the determination of the Exalted in Might, the Knowing.

items.verse: وَأَنَّا كُنَّا نَقْعُدُ مِنْهَا مَقَـٰعِدَ لِلسَّمْعِ ۖ فَمَن يَسْتَمِعِ ٱلْـَٔانَ يَجِدْ لَهُۥ شِهَابًۭا رَّصَدًۭا
items.verseEn: And we used to sit therein in positions for hearing, but whoever listens now will find a burning flame lying in wait for him.

categories.name: نَعِيمُ أَصْحَابِ ٱلْيَمِينِ فِي سُورَةِ ٱلْوَاقِعَةِ
categories.nameEn: Delights of the Companions of the Right in Surah Al-Waqi'ah

items.verseEn: And fruit of what they select
And the meat of fowl, from whatever they desire.
And [for them are] fair women with large, [beautiful] eyes,

categories.name: عَنَاصِرُ مِنْ قِصَّةِ أَيُّوبَ
categories.nameEn: Elements from the Story of Prophet Ayyub

items.verse: ۞ وَأَيُّوبَ إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُۥٓ أَنِّى مَسَّنِىَ ٱلضُّرُّ وَأَنتَ أَرْحَمُ ٱلرَّٰحِمِينَ
items.verseEn: And [mention] Job, when he called to his Lord, "Indeed, adversity has touched me, and You are the most merciful of the merciful."

items.en: (is a spring of) water to bathe
items.verse: ٱرْكُضْ بِرِجْلِكَ ۖ هَـٰذَا مُغْتَسَلٌۢ بَارِدٌۭ وَشَرَابٌۭ
items.verseEn: [So he was told], "Strike [the ground] with your foot; this is a [spring for a] cool bath and drink."

items.verse: وَخُذْ بِيَدِكَ ضِغْثًۭا فَٱضْرِب بِّهِۦ وَلَا تَحْنَثْ ۗ إِنَّا وَجَدْنَـٰهُ صَابِرًۭا ۚ نِّعْمَ ٱلْعَبْدُ ۖ إِنَّهُۥٓ أَوَّابٌۭ
items.verseEn: [We said], "And take in your hand a bunch [of grass] and strike with it and do not break your oath." Indeed, We found him patient, an excellent servant. Indeed, he was one repeatedly turning back [to Allāh].

items.verse: فَٱسْتَجَبْنَا لَهُۥ فَكَشَفْنَا مَا بِهِۦ مِن ضُرٍّۢ ۖ وَءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ أَهْلَهُۥ وَمِثْلَهُم مَّعَهُمْ رَحْمَةًۭ مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَذِكْرَىٰ لِلْعَـٰبِدِينَ
items.verseEn: So We responded to him and removed what afflicted him of adversity. And We gave him [back] his family and the like thereof with them as mercy from Us and a reminder for the worshippers [of Allāh].

harf.hint: The act of placing the forehead on the ground in worship.
harf.verse: 50:40 - And [in part] of the night exalt Him and after prostration [i.e., prayer].

deduction.title: The One Excluded by Water's Test
deduction.intro: In the annals of ancient scripture, there exists a fascinating figure whose story begins not with a dramatic miracle or a thunderous revelation, but with a simple yet profound trial of faith. A nation stood at a crossroads, seeking divine guidance for leadership. What unfolded was a test so clever that it would reveal the true believers among them. Can you unravel this mystery from the clues left behind?
clues: I came from a lineage the people admired, and Allah chose me for a sacred purpose they could not comprehend.
My body carried a mark of distinction that others lacked, making me stand apart among my people.
When the river was declared the test, most turned back—but I pressed forward, driven by unwavering faith in Allah's promise.
I was commanded to never drink from forbidden waters, yet one drop escaped my lips—a sin that haunted my heart forever.
Allah destined me to meet a young shepherd who would one day become legendary for slaying a mighty adversary.
When the armies clashed, I fell not by the enemy's hand, but by divine decree as punishment for my disobedience at the river.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Talut (Saul) – The First King of Bani Israel
Prophet Dawud (David) – The Shepherd King
Goliath (Jalut) – The Giant of Bani Israel
Prophet Sulayman (Solomon) – The Master of Wind and Jinn
King Saul's General Who Carried the Ark
identity.answer: Talut (Saul) – The First King of Bani Israel

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Refusing to drink from the Nile River during Exodus
Testing the army by commanding them not to drink from a sacred river
Building the Temple of Jerusalem in forty years
Fighting the Amalekites with Prophet Musa
Constructing a palace for Bilqis – Queen of Sheba
trial.answer: Testing the army by commanding them not to drink from a sacred river

location.label: Key Element
options: The River Jordan near Jerusalem
The river at the border of the Promised Land
The Euphrates River in Babylon
The Nile River in Egypt
The waters surrounding the city of Jericho
location.answer: The river at the border of the Promised Land

outcome.label: Outcome
options: I became the greatest king and built Jerusalem's temple
I fell in battle as punishment for my one act of disobedience
I was saved by my faith and became a prophet
My army conquered the entire known world
I lived to see my son Solomon inherit my kingdom
outcome.answer: I fell in battle as punishment for my one act of disobedience

deduction.arabic: فَلَمَّا فَصَلَ طَالُوتُ بِٱلْجُنُودِ قَالَ إِنَّ ٱللَّهَ مُبْتَلِيكُم بِنَهَرٍۢ فَمَن شَرِبَ مِنْهُ فَلَيْسَ مِنِّى وَمَن لَّمْ يَطْعَمْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ مِنِّىٓ إِلَّا مَنِ ٱغْتَرَفَ غُرْفَةًۢ بِيَدِهِۦ ۚ فَشَرِبُوا۟ مِنْهُ إِلَّا قَلِيلًۭا مِّنْهُمْ ۚ فَلَمَّا جَاوَزَهُۥ هُوَ وَٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوا۟ مَعَهُۥ قَالُوا۟ لَا طَاقَةَ لَنَا ٱلْيَوْمَ بِجَالُوتَ وَجُنُودِهِۦ ۚ قَالَ ٱلَّذِينَ يَظُنُّونَ أَنَّهُم مُّلَـٰقُوا۟ ٱللَّهِ كَم مِّن فِئَةٍۢ قَلِيلَةٍ غَلَبَتْ فِئَةًۭ كَثِيرَةًۢ بِإِذْنِ ٱللَّهِ ۗ وَٱللَّهُ مَعَ ٱلصَّـٰبِرِينَ
deduction.verse: And when Saul went forth with the soldiers, he said, "Indeed, Allāh will be testing you with a river. So whoever drinks from it is not of me, and whoever does not taste it is indeed of me, excepting one who takes [from it] in the hollow of his hand." But they drank from it, except a [very] few of them. Then when he had crossed it along with those who believed with him, they said, "There is no power for us today against Goliath and his soldiers." But those who were certain that they would meet Allāh said, "How many a small company has overcome a large company by permission of Allāh. And Allāh is with the patient." (2:249)

scramble.hint: This verse describes the immense value of Laylatul Qadr, the blessed night in the last ten nights of Ramadan, which is better than a thousand months of worship.
scramble.verseEn: And what can make you know what is the Night of Decree?
